### Parties E+W


11.6. — [ F28 (A1) Where the person who is the subject of proceedings is not the applicant and is a child, the court must consider, at every stage in the proceedings, whether to make that child a party to proceedings.


(For when a child should be made a party to proceedings generally see paragraph 7 of Practice Direction 16A). ]


(1) In proceedings under this Part, a person may file a Part 18 application notice for that person or another person to—


(2) As soon as practicable after receiving an application under paragraph (1), the court must do one of the following—


(3) The court officer must inform the following persons of the court's action under paragraph (2)—


(4) The court may at any time direct—


and such a direction may be made by the court of its own initiative as well as upon an application under paragraph (1).


(5) Where the court directs the addition or removal of a party, it may give consequential directions about—


Textual Amendments


F28 Rule 11.6(A1) inserted (17.7.2015) by The Family Procedure (Amendment No. 2) Rules 2015 (S.I. 2015/1420) , rules 1(2) , 9(a)


F29 Words in rule 11.6(3)(b) omitted (17.7.2015) by virtue of The Family Procedure (Amendment No. 2) Rules 2015 (S.I. 2015/1420) , rules 1(2) , 9(b)


F30 Words in rule 11.6(5)(a) omitted (17.7.2015) by virtue of The Family Procedure (Amendment No. 2) Rules 2015 (S.I. 2015/1420) , rules 1(2) , 9(c)


### Hearings and service of orders E+W


11.7. —(1) Any hearing relating to an application for a F31 ... protection order must be in private unless the court otherwise directs.


(2) The court may direct the withholding of any submissions made, or any evidence adduced, for or at any hearing in proceedings to which this Part applies—

metadata.title: Part 11 — Applications under Part 4A of the Family Law Act 1996 or Part 1 of Schedule 2 to the Female Genital Mutilation Act 2003
